description: Route policy manifest for the FastAPI backend. Every route in app.api.v1 MUST have an entry here. The pytest at tests/test_route_policy_manifest.py fails on (a) any new route lacking a manifest entry, (b) any manifest entry whose route no longer exists, (c) any entry with a TBD policy field, and (d) any entry missing the must_change_password_allowed boolean. Edit by hand; do not auto-regenerate after the M0 seed. The must_change_password_allowed flag mirrors the allow-list in app.api.v1.auth._PASSWORD_GATE_ALLOWED_PATHS — only /auth/me and /auth/set-password are reachable while a user's must_change_password flag is True.

routes.rate_limit_rule: none (auth-gated; M3 adds AI-heavy + export limits)
routes.error_lang: es_partial (raw service exceptions can leak; M2 will normalize)

routes.notes: Reads the immutable audit_log itself; never mutates.
Search across every submission CheckWise stores. Read-only.

routes.error_lang: es_partial (raw service exceptions can leak; M2 will normalize)

routes.audit_rule: not_logged (preview is a hot read; full download writes the audit row)

routes.tenant_rule: scoped by client_admin membership in the search service (filters to vendors of the caller's client_ids)
routes.notes: Search submissions visible to the caller's client_admin role.

routes.tenant_rule: scoped to the active workspace's vendor by the search service
routes.notes: Search submissions belonging to the active portal workspace.

routes.audit_rule: logged on ?download=1 (provider.document_downloaded); inline preview not logged
sampled (token resolution audited on POST /unlock only)
